sections.paragraph:
fut. apoc. יִדֶף-

(1) pr. to cast, to throw, i.q. רָמָה, ῥίπτω, whence pr.n. רָפָה (casting forth, throwing down), specially-

(2) to cast down, to let fall, especially the hand, die Hand fallen laffen, see Piel, Hiphil. In Kal intrans. to decline as the day, i.e. to draw to a close, Jdg 19:9 used of hay in the fire, i.e. to sink down, Isai. 5:24 followed by מִן to relax, to desist from any person or thing, Exo 4:26 Jud. 8:3 Neh 6:9.

(3) to be let down, especially of the hand, 2Ch 15:7, אַל־יִרְפּוּ יְדֵיכֶם “let not your hands hang down,” i.e. be not lazy in the work. Relaxed hands are very often ascribed to those who have lost their courage. 2Sa 4:1, וַיִּרְפּוּ יָדָיו “and his hands were let down,” his courage was gone, Isa 13:7 Jer 6:24 50:43 Eze 7:17 21:12 Zep 3:16. Used also of a person himself, Jer 49:24, רָֽפְתָה דַמֶּשֶׂק “Damascus (i.e. its inhabitants) has become fainthearted.”

Niphal, to be slack, remiss, idle, Exo 5:8, 17 .

Piel, to let down, e.g. wings, Eze 1:24, 25 Eze 1:25a girdle (i.e. to loosen), Job 12:21. Specially to let down any one’s hands, i.q. to destroy his courage (compare Kal No. 2), Jer 38:4 Ezr 4:4.

Hiphil, הִרְפָּה, imp. and fut. apoc. הֶרֶף and יֶרֶף-

(1) to let down the hand. 2Sa 24:16, הֶרֶף יָדֶ֑ךָ “let down thy hand,” i.e. stop from inflicting plagues. Followed by מִן for, to desert, to forsake any one, Jos 10:6. Without יָד, to let down (the hand), i.e. to desist, followed by מִן from any person or thing, Jud. 11:37 Deu 9:14.

(2) to leave off any thing, e.g. a work begun, Neh 6:3 instruction, Pro 4:13 any person, i.e. to desert him, i.q. עָזַב Neh 6:3 Deuteron. 4:31 31:6 , Deu 31:8 Jos 1:5 Psa 138:8. Absol. Psa 46:11, “leave off (your own attempts) and know.” 1Sa 15:16.

(3) to let any one go (opp. to, to lay hold of, to detain); followed by an acc. Son 3:4 Job 7:19 27:6 .

Hithpael, to shew oneself remiss-(a) to be lazy, Job 18:3 Pro 18:9.-(b) to lose one’s courage, Pro 24:10.

Derivatives, רָפֶה, רִפְֹון.

II.

רפה

stands not unfrequently for רָפָא to heal, (compare קָרָא No. II.=קָרָה). In this sense there occurs imp. רְפָה (for רְפָא) Psa 60:4 fut. תִּרְפֶּינָה Job 5:18.

Niphal, נִרְפָּתָה Jer 51:9 inf. הֵרָפֵה Jer 19:11 fut. יֵרָפוּ. 2Ki 2:22.

Piel, יְרַפּוּ Jer 6:14.

רפה

(“casting down,” “throwing down,” i.e. a hero, a champion, a giant) [Raphah], pr.n.-

(1) of an ancient giant, whose descendants, who were also giants, were called יְלִידֵי הָרָפָה and רְפָאִים; see that word.-(2) 1Ch 8:37 for which there is 1Ch 9:43, רְפָיָה [Rephaiah], (“whom Jehovah healed”).
